statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simplify a complex fraction, we find the common denominator of all fractions. In this case, it is {y\mathopen{}\left(y+4\right)}. We multiply the complex fractions numerator and denominator by this common denominator, and we have:
Since y and y+4 were canceled, y \ne 0 and \ne -4 are lost domain conditions.
